# The Brain

![The Brain](assets/brain.png)
*Image generated by Pollinations.ai (free, no API key) ÔÇö automatically offloaded from Claude to save tokens.*

> Claude orchestrates. Free AI does the heavy lifting. Git remembers everything.

## Why I built this

I was paying $20/month for Claude Pro and kept running out of tokens ÔÇö it felt like 1998 AOL dial-up screeching to a halt mid-sentence. So I built The Brain: an orchestration system that offloads as much AI work as possible to free providers like Groq, Gemini, Mistral, and Cerebras, while Claude handles only the decisions and orchestration. Now I get smart AI assistance without the token anxiety. No more dial-up drama.

---

**The Brain** is an AI orchestration system where Claude acts as the intelligent dispatcher, routing every task to the cheapest and most capable AI provider available ÔÇö saving Claude tokens for decisions, planning, and conversation only.

```
You Ôćĺ Orchestrator (Claude) Ôćĺ Router
                                 Ôćô
              ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ
              Ôöé  classification  Ôćĺ  Cerebras    (FREE, ~1500 tok/s)  Ôöé
              Ôöé  factual Q&A     Ôćĺ  Cerebras    (FREE, ~1500 tok/s)  Ôöé
              Ôöé  summarisation   Ôćĺ  Gemini      (FREE, 1M context)   Ôöé
              Ôöé  extraction      Ôćĺ  Mistral     (FREE)               Ôöé
              Ôöé  translation     Ôćĺ  Gemini      (FREE)               Ôöé
              Ôöé  coding          Ôćĺ  Mistral     (FREE) Ôćĺ Fireworks   Ôöé
              Ôöé  reasoning       Ôćĺ  SambaNova   (FREE 70B)           Ôöé
              Ôöé  creative        Ôćĺ  Mistral     (FREE) Ôćĺ SambaNova   Ôöé
              Ôöé  general         Ôćĺ  Cerebras    (FREE) Ôćĺ Groq        Ôöé
              Ôöé  image           Ôćĺ  Pollinations (no key)            Ôöé
              ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś
                                 Ôćô
                         stats/usage.json
                                 Ôćô
                    stats/usage.json Ôćĺ run update_readme_stats.py to refresh
```

---

## Why this architecture

| Problem | Solution |
|---|---|
| Claude tokens are expensive | Route simple tasks to free models |
| Different AIs excel at different tasks | Smart routing table per task type |
| Hard to track what was used | Every call logged to stats/usage.json |
| Want to know savings | Nightly report shows tokens saved vs Claude cost |

---

## Active Providers

### Free ÔÇö API key required (no credit card)

| Provider | Model | Best for | Speed | Sign up |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Cerebras** | Llama 3.1 8B | Classification, scoring, yes/no | ~284ms | [cloud.cerebras.ai](https://cloud.cerebras.ai) |
| **Groq** | Llama 3.1 8B Instant | Factual Q&A, general tasks | ~366ms | [console.groq.com](https://console.groq.com) |
| **Gemini** | 2.5 Flash Lite | Summarisation, long text (1M context), translation | ~541ms | [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey) |
| **Mistral** | Mistral Small | Coding, creative writing, extraction | ~1172ms | [console.mistral.ai](https://console.mistral.ai) |
| **HuggingFace** | Llama 3 8B | Open-source fallback | ~924ms | [huggingface.co/settings/tokens](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens) |
| **SambaNova** | Llama 3.3 70B | High-quality free inference | ~11s | [cloud.sambanova.ai](https://cloud.sambanova.ai) |
| **Fireworks AI** | DeepSeek V3 | Fast general inference, free tier | ~1963ms | [fireworks.ai](https://fireworks.ai) |
| **Cloudflare Workers AI** | llama-3.1-8b-instruct | General, classification | Ôťô Free (no CC) | [dash.cloudflare.com](https://dash.cloudflare.com) |
| **OpenRouter** | llama-3.1-8b-instruct:free | General fallback, many models | Ôťô Free tier | [openrouter.ai](https://openrouter.ai) |
| **OpenAI** | GPT-4o-mini | Coding, structured output, general tasks | ~2372ms | [platform.openai.com](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys) |

### Free ÔÇö no key, no sign-up

| Provider | Capability | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Pollinations.ai** | Text + Image generation | HTTP GET only, zero setup, FLUX model for images |

---

## Providers NOT in use ÔÇö and why

| Provider | Reason |
|---|---|
| **xAI (Grok)** | Free API tier removed in 2026 |
| **Cohere** | API key was never properly configured (placeholder value) |
| **OpenRouter (free models)** | Free tier models rotate frequently ÔÇö keep as fallback only |
| **DeepSeek** | Key is valid but account has no balance |
| **Together AI** | Credit limit exceeded ÔÇö needs payment added |
| **Anthropic (direct)** | Key not configured ÔÇö would be paid anyway; Claude is already the orchestrator |
| **Ollama** | Local-only, requires local installation ÔÇö no cloud option |
| **DuckDuckGo AI** | Officially blocked/archived as of January 2026 |
| **GPT4Free (g4f)** | Providers break daily ÔÇö too fragile for a stable system |
| **mlvoca.com** | Unproven, very new service |

---

## Quick start

### 1. Clone and install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/SoylentAquamarine/the-brain.git
cd the-brain
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 2. Configure API keys

```bash
# Edit .env and paste in your keys
GROQ_API_KEY=...
GEMINI_API_KEY=...
MISTRAL_API_KEY=...
CEREBRAS_API_KEY=...
HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY=...
SAMBANOVA_API_KEY=...
FIREWORKS_API_KEY=...
OPENAI_API_KEY=...
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=...
CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY=...  # format: accountID:apiToken ÔÇö get both from dash.cloudflare.com
```

### 3. Offload a task

#### Cerebras ÔÇö fastest inference (~1500 tok/s), best for classification
**Available models:** `llama3.1-8b` (default) ┬Ě `qwen-3-235b-a22b-instruct-2507` ┬Ě `gpt-oss-120b`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider cerebras --type classification --prompt "Is this positive or negative: 'Great product!'"
python delegate.py --provider cerebras --type factual_qa     --prompt "What is the capital of France?"
```

#### Groq ÔÇö very fast, great all-rounder
**Available models:** `llama-3.1-8b-instant` (default) ┬Ě `meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct` ┬Ě `qwen/qwen3-32b` ┬Ě `groq/compound`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider groq --type factual_qa   --prompt "What year was Python created?"
python delegate.py --provider groq --type general      --prompt "Explain REST APIs in plain English"
```

#### Gemini ÔÇö huge context window (1M tokens), best for long documents
**Available models:** `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` (default) ┬Ě `gemini-2.5-flash` ┬Ě `gemini-2.5-pro` ┬Ě `gemini-2.0-flash` ┬Ě `gemma-3-27b-it`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider gemini --type summarization --prompt "Summarise this 50-page document: ..."
python delegate.py --provider gemini --type translation   --prompt "Translate to Spanish: ..."
```

#### Mistral ÔÇö best free model for coding and creative writing
**Available models:** `mistral-small-latest` (default) ┬Ě `mistral-medium-latest` ┬Ě `codestral-latest` ┬Ě `devstral-latest` ┬Ě `open-mistral-nemo`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider mistral --type coding    --prompt "Write a Python function that sorts a list of dicts by key"
python delegate.py --provider mistral --type creative  --prompt "Write a cover letter for a software engineer role at Stripe"
python delegate.py --provider mistral --type extraction --prompt "Extract all skills mentioned in this job posting: ..."
```

#### OpenAI ÔÇö strong all-rounder, free tier
**Available models:** `gpt-4o-mini` (default) ┬Ě `gpt-4o` ┬Ě `gpt-5.4-mini` ┬Ě `gpt-5.4` ┬Ě `gpt-3.5-turbo`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider openai --type coding    --prompt "Write a regex to validate email addresses"
python delegate.py --provider openai --type reasoning --prompt "What are the trade-offs between SQL and NoSQL databases?"
```

#### HuggingFace ÔÇö open-source model variety, free fallback
**Available models:** `meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct` (default) ┬Ě `mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3` ┬Ě `HuggingFaceH4/zephyr-7b-beta`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider huggingface --type general      --prompt "Summarise the key points of this paragraph: ..."
python delegate.py --provider huggingface --type factual_qa   --prompt "What is machine learning?"
```

#### SambaNova ÔÇö free 70B model, highest quality of the free providers
**Available models:** `Meta-Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct` (default) ┬Ě `Llama-4-Maverick-17B-128E-Instruct` ┬Ě `DeepSeek-V3.1` ┬Ě `DeepSeek-V3.2` ┬Ě `gemma-3-12b-it`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider sambanova --type reasoning   --prompt "Analyse the pros and cons of microservices architecture"
python delegate.py --provider sambanova --type creative    --prompt "Write a detailed blog post about AI orchestration"
```

#### Fireworks ÔÇö fast inference, free tier
**Available models:** `accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v3p1` (default) ┬Ě `accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v3p2` ┬Ě `accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p5` ┬Ě `accounts/fireworks/models/gpt-oss-120b`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider fireworks --type coding   --prompt "Write a Python class for a binary search tree"
python delegate.py --provider fireworks --type general  --prompt "What are the SOLID principles?"
```

#### Cloudflare Workers AI ÔÇö edge-hosted, fast, free tier
**Available models:** `@cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct` (default) ┬Ě `@cf/meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct-fp8-fast`
**Key format:** Set `CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY=accountID:apiToken` in .env
```bash
python delegate.py --provider cloudflare --type general        --prompt "Explain what an API is"
python delegate.py --provider cloudflare --type classification --prompt "Classify as question or statement: 'What time is it?'"
```

#### OpenRouter ÔÇö gateway to hundreds of models, free tier available
**Available models:** `meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct:free` (default) ┬Ě `google/gemma-3-27b-it:free` ┬Ě `mistralai/mistral-small-3.1-24b-instruct:free`
```bash
python delegate.py --provider openrouter --type general  --prompt "What is the difference between TCP and UDP?"
```

#### Pollinations ÔÇö no key needed, text and images
```bash
# Text
python delegate.py --provider pollinations --type general  --prompt "Write a haiku about coding"
# Image ÔÇö use the adapter directly
python -c "from brain.adapters.pollinations_adapter import PollinationsAdapter; ..."
```

#### Override the model for any provider
```bash
# Use Groq with the larger Llama 4 Scout model
GROQ_MODEL=meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct python delegate.py --provider groq --type reasoning --prompt "..."

# Use Mistral's code-specific model
MISTRAL_MODEL=codestral-latest python delegate.py --provider mistral --type coding --prompt "..."

# Use Gemini Pro instead of Flash Lite
GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-2.5-pro python delegate.py --provider gemini --type summarization --prompt "..."
```

### 4. Check status

```bash
python status.py
```

### 5. View usage report

```bash
python report.py
```

---

## Routing guide

| Task | Use | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Classify, label, yes/no | `cerebras` | Fastest (~1500 tok/s), free |
| Quick factual Q&A | `groq` | Fast, reliable, free |
| Summarise long text | `gemini` | 1M token context, free |
| Code generation | `mistral` | Best free coding model |
| Creative writing / drafting | `mistral` | Best quality output, free |
| Translation | `gemini` | Strongest multilingual, free |
| Image generation | `pollinations` | No key needed |
| High-quality, no rush | `sambanova` | 70B model, free, slower |
| General fallback | `fireworks` | DeepSeek V3, free |
| General fallback (GPT-4o-mini) | `openai` | Strong all-rounder, free tier |

---

## Project structure

```
the-brain/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ delegate.py              # Call this to offload a task to a free AI worker
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ status.py                # Live provider dashboard + usage stats
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ report.py                # Usage report (human-readable)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ update_readme_stats.py   # Auto-update this README with stats
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ INTEGRATION.md           # How to wire a new workflow window into the-brain
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ brain/
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ orchestrator.py      # Core dispatcher with automatic fallback
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ router.py            # Routing table (task type Ôćĺ provider order)
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ stats.py             # Persistent usage tracking
Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ adapters/            # One file per provider
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ stats/usage.json         # Running usage log (auto-created)
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ assets/brain.png
```

## Using Claude Code with The Brain

This section explains how to wire up your own local Claude Code instance so it
automatically offloads AI tasks to free workers ÔÇö the same setup used to build
and maintain this project.

### What is Claude Code?

Claude Code is Anthropic's CLI tool that runs Claude directly in your terminal
with full access to your file system, git, and shell. Unlike the web chat, it
can execute commands, read and write files, and follow persistent instructions
stored in a `CLAUDE.md` file in your project.

Download it from: **[claude.ai/code](https://claude.ai/code)**

**Does it work on the free tier?**
No ÔÇö Claude Code requires a Claude Pro subscription ($20/month) or an Anthropic
API key with credits. But that is exactly why this project exists. With The Brain
doing the heavy lifting, Claude handles almost nothing directly ÔÇö it just decides
who to call and relays the result. In a typical session, 100% of AI tokens go to
free workers. You pay $20/month for the orchestrator; the actual work is free.

---

### Step 1 ÔÇö Clone the repo and install dependencies

```bash
git clone https://github.com/SoylentAquamarine/the-brain.git
cd the-brain
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

---

### Step 2 ÔÇö Add your API keys

Copy the example env file and fill in your keys. All providers listed have
free tiers ÔÇö no credit card required for most of them.

```bash
# Create your .env file
cp .env.example .env   # or create it manually

# Add keys for the providers you want ÔÇö you don't need all of them
GROQ_API_KEY=...        # https://console.groq.com
GEMINI_API_KEY=...      # https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
MISTRAL_API_KEY=...     # https://console.mistral.ai
CEREBRAS_API_KEY=...    # https://cloud.cerebras.ai
HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY=... # https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens
SAMBANOVA_API_KEY=...   # https://cloud.sambanova.ai
FIREWORKS_API_KEY=...   # https://fireworks.ai
OPENAI_API_KEY=...      # https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=...  # https://openrouter.ai
CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY=...  # format: accountID:apiToken ÔÇö get both from dash.cloudflare.com
```

Even with just one or two keys you get meaningful offloading. Groq + Gemini
covers most use cases for free.

---

### Step 3 ÔÇö Open the project in Claude Code

```bash
cd the-brain
claude
```

That's the key step. When Claude Code starts inside a directory it
**automatically reads `CLAUDE.md`** and follows it as standing instructions for
the entire session. The `CLAUDE.md` in this repo tells Claude to:

- Route classification and scoring Ôćĺ Cerebras
- Route summarisation Ôćĺ Gemini
- Route coding and creative writing Ôćĺ Mistral
- Route analysis and reasoning Ôćĺ SambaNova
- Route general questions Ôćĺ Groq
- Keep only git, file I/O, and orchestration decisions for itself

You don't have to configure anything else. Open the folder, Claude picks up
the instructions and starts offloading automatically.

---

### Step 4 ÔÇö Verify it is working

Type `status` in the Claude Code window:

```
status
```

You should see a dashboard showing which providers are online and any usage
stats accumulated so far. If all your keys are valid, all providers will show
as `[online]`.

To test a live offload:

```bash
python delegate.py --provider groq --type factual_qa --prompt "What is 2+2?"
```

---

### Step 5 ÔÇö Update the README with your usage stats

Every `delegate.py` call writes to `stats/usage.json` locally. To publish your
stats to GitHub:

```bash
python update_readme_stats.py
git add stats/usage.json README.md && git commit -m "chore: update stats" && git push
```

---

### How CLAUDE.md works ÔÇö the short version

Any file named `CLAUDE.md` in a project directory (or any parent directory) is
automatically loaded by Claude Code at session start. It acts like a permanent
system prompt for that project. You can:

- Tell Claude which tools to use
- Define workflows and shortcuts (`status` Ôćĺ run `python status.py`)
- Set routing rules (what to offload, what to keep)
- Document project conventions

Editing `CLAUDE.md` and reopening the project is all it takes to change
Claude's default behaviour for that window. The offloading rules in this repo's
`CLAUDE.md` are a working example ÔÇö copy and adapt them for your own projects.

---


---

## Changelog

<!-- BRAIN_CHANGELOG_START -->
### 2026-05-02 ÔÇö MCP server, stats decoupling, session lifecycle hooks

- **MCP server** (`mcp_server.py`) ÔÇö exposes `delegate` as a native Claude Code tool alongside Bash/Read/Edit; offloading now happens automatically rather than relying on CLAUDE.md instructions. Install dep: `pip install mcp`
- **`push_stats.py`** ÔÇö standalone script to update README stats and push to GitHub; completely decoupled from health checks so stats publish at every session closeout regardless of Task Scheduler state
- **`session_start.py`** updated ÔÇö briefing now includes a usage summary line (total calls, tokens offloaded, cost, time since last call)
- **`C:/Claude/git/CLAUDE.md`** updated ÔÇö `session_start.py` runs at every session open; `push_stats.py` runs at every session close, covering all projects
- **Root cause identified**: `TheBrainHealthCheck` Task Scheduler task has a broken Python path (`YOUR_USER` placeholder) ÔÇö has been failing silently every hour

### 2026-05-01
- **Ollama adapter** (`brain/adapters/ollama/adapter.py`) ÔÇö LAN/local inference via Ollama REST API; zero extra dependencies (stdlib `urllib`). Reads `OLLAMA_HOST`/`OLLAMA_PORT` from `.env`, queries `/api/tags` on startup to auto-discover installed models, skips embedding-only models.
- **Auto model selection** ÔÇö priority order: `qwen2.5-coder:7b` Ôćĺ `deepseek-coder:6.7b` Ôćĺ `codellama:13b` Ôćĺ `qwen2.5:7b` Ôćĺ `llama3.1` Ôćĺ `mistral` Ôćĺ `phi3` Ôćĺ `llama3.2`. Falls back gracefully if the server is unreachable.
- **Routing** ÔÇö `ollama` added to all 9 fallback chains via `sync_docs.py`; supports coding, reasoning, general, factual_qa, summarization, extraction, creative.
- **CLAUDE.md** updated automatically with new provider row and chain positions.

### 2026-04-25 (session 3)
- **GitHub Pages** ÔÇö Created and launched: [soylentaquamarine.github.io/the-brain](https://soylentaquamarine.github.io/the-brain/)

### 2026-04-25 (session 2)
- **Adapter model audit & fixes** ÔÇö parallel health test across all 12 providers / 30 models; pruned dead models, fixed broken ones.
- **OpenAI o-series fix** ÔÇö `o4-mini` now uses `max_completion_tokens` with 1024 minimum so reasoning tokens don't exhaust the budget and return empty content.
- **Groq cleanup** ÔÇö removed decommissioned `gemma2-9b-it` model.
- **Cerebras cleanup** ÔÇö removed `llama3.1-70b` (404); single confirmed-live model `llama3.1-8b` remains.
- **SambaNova cleanup** ÔÇö removed unavailable `DeepSeek-V3-0324`; `Llama-4-Maverick-17B-128E-Instruct` confirmed live.
- **Unicode fix in `delegate.py`** ÔÇö all stdout now UTF-8 encoded; fixes crash on Windows cp1252 when provider responses contain arrows or emoji.

### 2026-04-25 (session 1)
- **Live README health dashboard** ÔÇö `update_readme_stats.py` injects a 24h provider health table with uptime/latency; `health_check.py` auto-pushes to GitHub every hour.
- **MRTG-style health log rollup** (`brain/health_rollup.py`) ÔÇö keeps last 24h at full resolution, 6h bucket averages for 24hÔÇô7d, 24h buckets for 7d+. Reduces 879 entries to ~300, stays small forever. Fully dynamic ÔÇö no hardcoded provider list.
- **Phase 4: Prompt cache layer** (`brain/cache.py`) ÔÇö SHA-256 keyed on prompt+provider+type, 24h TTL, 500-entry LRU eviction. 0ms / 0 tokens on cache hits. `--no-cache` flag to bypass.
- **Phase 2: Parallel execution** (`orchestrator.run_parallel`) ÔÇö races N providers simultaneously via `ThreadPoolExecutor`, returns first success. `delegate.py --parallel N` flag.
- **`delegate.py` routes through `Orchestrator`** ÔÇö gains automatic fallback on every call instead of hard-failing on first provider error.
- **Phase 1: Time-based provider cooldowns** ÔÇö replaced permanent session-disable with timed cooldowns: rate-limit=5min, timeout=3min, model-error=2min. Auth errors remain session-permanent.
- **Phase 1: Health-based adaptive routing** (`router._reorder_by_health`) ÔÇö demotes providers with <60% 24h uptime to end of fallback chain, reads from `health_log.json` dynamically.
- **Dynamic provider tiers** ÔÇö `update_readme_stats.py` reads tiers from REGISTRY; new providers appear in README automatically.

### 2026-04-24
- **Removed nightly GitHub Action** ÔÇö replaced with Windows Task Scheduler AT job running `health_check.py` hourly locally.
- **Removed fragile health check infrastructure** ÔÇö previous GitHub Actions-based health checks were causing noise; rebuilt as local scheduled task.
- **Adapter plugin folder structure** (`brain/adapters/`) ÔÇö each provider is an isolated subfolder with its own `adapter.py`; auto-discovered by the registry. Adding a new provider = one new folder.
- **Router as sole routing authority** ÔÇö all provider ordering logic consolidated into `router.py`; orchestrator delegates entirely to it.
- **Health log feeds router** ÔÇö `router.py` reads `health_log.json` to reorder providers by live uptime rather than static rules alone.

### 2026-04-22 ÔÇô 2026-04-23
- **Cloudflare Workers AI adapter** ÔÇö edge-hosted free inference, no credit card required.
- **Fireworks AI model fix** ÔÇö switched to `llama-v3p3-70b-instruct` after `v3p1` returned 404.
- **Cohere model fix** ÔÇö updated default to `command-r-08-2024` after alias retirement.
- **HuggingFace model fix** ÔÇö switched default to `Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct` (openly accessible free tier).
- **xAI removed** ÔÇö free API tier discontinued in 2026.
- **Cohere added** ÔÇö Command-R model for retrieval/extraction tasks.

### 2026-04-21
- **Hourly provider health checks** ÔÇö `health_check.py` pings every provider+model, scores response correctness, logs to `stats/health_log.json`. Health graph auto-generated.
- **`status.py` live dashboard** ÔÇö shows provider availability, usage stats, and session summary in terminal.
- **Maximum offloading policy** ÔÇö `CLAUDE.md` updated to enforce offloading for all non-trivial AI tasks.

### 2026-04-19 ÔÇô 2026-04-20
- **OpenRouter adapter** ÔÇö gateway to 50+ models, free tier available.
- **xAI (Grok) adapter** ÔÇö added (later removed when free tier was discontinued).
- **SambaNova adapter** ÔÇö 70B Llama, highest free-tier quality for reasoning tasks.
- **Fireworks AI adapter** ÔÇö DeepSeek V3 and Llama 70B, strong on coding.
- **OpenAI adapter** ÔÇö GPT-4o-mini, reclassified as free tier.

### 2026-04-18
- **Initial release** ÔÇö core orchestration system with `delegate.py`, `brain/orchestrator.py`, `brain/router.py`, `brain/stats.py`. Providers: Groq, Gemini, Mistral, Cerebras, HuggingFace, Pollinations, SambaNova.
- **Static routing table** ÔÇö curated fallback chains per task type (classification, coding, reasoning, etc.).
- **Persistent usage stats** ÔÇö every call logged to `stats/usage.json`; `report.py` for human-readable summary.
